channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Parked on zig zag, 5th April
item.description:
Illegally parked on zig zag by school

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Lewisham Road, Deptford, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Bulb has failed, 5th April
item.description:
The bulb seems to have failed and isn't lighting fully.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Lincoln Green Road, Poverest, Greater London

item.title: Recycling facility in Avenue Road Carpark needs emptying, 5th April
item.description:
The paper/cardboard recycling bins are filled to capacity and need emptying. (This photo was taken on Thursday 2nd April.)

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Blackthorn Grove, Welling, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dog fouling, 5th April
item.description:
The tenants of this property of where i have pin pointed the ground floor property have two dogs that they allow to poo on the grass areas around the estate
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Brodie Street, Old Kent Road, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Streatham High Road SW16 1PN number 63 ie Streath, 5th April
item.description:
Streatham High Road SW16 1PN number 63 ie Streatham Library at north end (ie not Pinfold Rd junction end) of frontage is a black painted doorway (fire exit?) which is tagged. Kindly NB – the graffiti filth above the door may be over 6 foot off the ground which if so is outside your contractor’s reach so you will need to put in a ‘special’ request eg like when a traffic sign get tagged (or just find a tall person or a paint brush with an extra long handle…..) Please forward fixmystreet.com link to your contractor for picture.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 63, Streatham High Road, Streatham, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: The bins are consistently overfilled, with large quantities of rubbish bags left on the ground., 5th April
item.description:
For several months now, both residential and commercial waste has been left overflowing in communal areas. The bins are consistently overfilled, with large quantities of rubbish bags left on the ground around them. As shown in the attached photographs, waste is not only spilling out of the bins but is also scattered across the surrounding area, including near residential doorways, walkways, and parking spaces. This ongoing situation has led to a significant increase in rat activity. The presence of food waste and unsecured rubbish is clearly attracting rodents, creating serious concerns for public health, hygiene, and safety It also poses a risk to children and pets who may come into contact with contaminated materials.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Stringers Close, Eltham
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dog fouling, 5th April
item.description:
The tenant at number 6 Clare House Coopers Road SE1 5JD keeps allowing his dog to poo inside the football pitch as well as on the grass outside number 6 and 5 Clare House. This has been happening for months and southwark street cleaners are having to clean up the mess. It’s also not fair on the cleaners as well as children that wish to play football inside the pitch. The tenant is currently in hospital however his son is there and is also allowing the dog to poo in the football pitch as well as on the grass. I’m fed up with smelling dog poo when I open my front door. Something needs to be done urgently
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Brodie Street, Old Kent Road, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dead Fox on pavement, 5th April
item.description:
A fox has been moved on to pavement at the top of Shooters Hill. If you are driving up from Well Hall end, fox will be on your right just past turning for Kennilworth gardens. If you are coming from Welling, it will be on your left before turning for Kennilworth Gardens. Its a young fox so not an adult.
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Shooters Hill, Plumstead
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Leigham Court Road SW16 / Valley Road / Knolly’s Road, 5th April
item.description:
Leigham Court Road SW16 / Valley Road / Knolly’s Road junction a number of polycarbonate A3 size flyposter boards attached to traffic light poles Attached with plastic zip ties so you will need a knife or a box cutter to remove.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Valley Road, Streatham Hill, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Streatham Common North SW16 / Leigham Court Road, 5th April
item.description:
Streatham Common North SW16 / Leigham Court Road junction A3 size flyposters attached to traffic light poles, streetlight poles and pedestrian footpath guard rails around this junction – polycarbonate boards secured with plastic zip ties so will need a knife and ‘snipes’ to remove.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Streatham Common North, West Norwood, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Intrusive light spill / light nuisance for streetlamp #8, 5th April
item.description:
This streetlamp sends direct and bright LED light into our living room. Can we get a shield or blocker installed to prevent the light spill at night while still lighting the sidewalk and street?

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Bayston Road, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Tree overhanging garden, 5th April
item.description:
Tree branches growing into our garden and keep falling off. I have a 2 year old toddler and a 9 year old.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Axminster Crescent, East Wickham, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.description:
Cars blocking driveway on double yellow blips
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Kyverdale Road, Hackney, London

item.title: Loop Rd - failed surfacing, 5th April
item.description:
10 - 20m either side of pin, wearing course failed, down to sub base in a large number of areas.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Loop Road, Chislehurst, Greater London

item.title: Used furniture near to Bin area and the entrance of creasy estate (1-7), 5th April
item.description:
Disturbs resident and blocked the footpath

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 1, Aberdour Street, Walworth, Greater London

item.title: Chislehurst Rd - open feeder pillar, 5th April
item.description:
Chislehurst Rd j/w Crofton La, as per pin on map, large feeder pillar - door completely off, wires exposed.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Chislehurst Road, Petts Wood, Greater London

item.title: Fridge dumped outside 19 Adolphus Road, 5th April
item.description:
Fridge dumped outside 19 Adolphus Road
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Adolphus Road, Hackney, London

item.title: Over 20 cars parked on curb blocking street, 5th April
item.description:
There is something going on in Kempe hall and the people are parking on pavement and on peoples drives and on there grass outside their houses

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Charnwood Road, Enfield
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Car parked over pavement, 5th April
item.description:
This car is always parked half in a drive and 90% across the public pavement. This has continued to stop people from using the pavement which causes us to have to walk into the road unnecessarily. This is very dangerous especially when we are trying to get our children to school.
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Wynford Way, Eltham
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Wilful obstruction of the highway with cones (Linked to Beechbrook site activity), 5th April
item.description:
There is a wilful obstruction of the highway on Lubbock Road. Two private 'No Parking' cones have been placed on the public carriageway. While positioned on the opposite side of the road and slightly downstream from the main entrance of Beechbrook (BR7 5JL), they appear to be placed by the site's responsible party to manage traffic or parking. This follows a pattern of highway obstructions related to this development, including previous issues with barriers (Ref: 9212605 and Ref: 9208929). The deliberate placement of these cones restricts public parking and creates a hazard on this narrow stretch, especially given the proximity to marked-out potholes. Please investigate this breach of the Highways Act 1980 and ensure the party at Beechbrook is instructed to remove them. Evidence Attached: LBB022-A, B, C: Photos clearly showing the "No Parking" cones and their relation to the road width.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Lubbock Road, Elmstead, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet
